channel.title: About on Matthew J Harmon .com
channel.description: Recent content in About on Matthew J Harmon .com

item.title: Media
item.description: Media These are made possible by the amazing people at the Internet Archive. Please consider exploring what they have to offer and if you like it sign up and donate. On KSTP discussing breaches. Discussing ATM skimmers on KSTP.

item.title: Upgrade
item.description: KMSP42 Site Upgrade Philosophy: Assume compromised and hostile. Compromised and each layer has been breached. Network hostile until proven otherwise. Data Rack ULTRABLOK CyberPower Sinewave UPS PFC Tripp Lite ISOBAR Surge Protector on each row. Fiber Switch at KMSP42 OpenBSD Router pcengines APU2 APU2 3 x Intel I210 options, packet reassembly, normalization, pf minefield to block bucket pf block table “Five hundred or five million” Border NTP anycast, stratum 1, wide variety of methods of NTP Stray DNS traffic redirect to local Unbound Arguments against "Is breaking DNS and possibly incurring the wrath of someone trying to run a bitcoin miner pool worth it?

item.title: 0xCAFE
item.description: Night Time Coffee Series Cross Industry Pairings Sets and tracks: - CompTIA A+ and Network + - CompTIA Security+ and Critical Security Controls - Filesystem Forensics

item.title: Projects
item.description: KMSP42 Network Walkthrough Night Time Coffee Series 59525.114837197405 2021-11-07T02:45:21.933851 2021310 W44 @156.493

Last updated: November 06, 2021 ToS;DR: Preface This Privacy Policy describes Our policies and procedures on the collection, use, and disclosure of Your information when You use the Service and tells You about Your privacy rights and how the law protects You. The below text is to satisfy both your user expectations and the lawyers; however, to be clear of our intent: This site doesn’t store your data, period. We own no servers, have no database, no logs, and publish in Static HTML.

item.title: Contact
item.description: No audio? I can respect that. It's a great song though. Email: wxe5aix9@duck.com Phone: +1-6129870115 Mailing Address Minneapolis, Minnesota United States Social Twitter Keybase Security Concern? Security.txt Humans.txt TOS;DR.txt TOSDR Entry

item.title: Matthew J. Harmon
item.description: About Matthew As a technologist since the ’90s, I have a strong track history of building resilient infrastructure and teaching the next generation about technology. I teach offensive and defensive cyber security and UNIX, including Linux, FreeBSD, OpenBSD, and HardenedBSD. Additionally, from time to time, I publish about cyber security, give interviews to local news stations, and present my research in public forums. Early on in my career, I was active as an international standards developer, functioning as a liaison from ANSI to ISO and the ITU.
